title: Multi-Residential Financing in the GTA: What Lenders Look For

blocks.text: The underwriting boxes GTA lenders check first — and the gaps that quietly kill otherwise strong applications. Multi-residential remains the most sought-after asset class in the region, which means lenders can afford to be selective.
The first three things a credit desk checks

items: Debt service coverage on in-place income — not projected rents
The sponsor's track record with this asset class and this market
Vacancy and turnover assumptions against the building's actual history

blocks.text: The most common killers are quiet ones: rent rolls that don't reconcile to bank deposits, deferred maintenance that surfaces in the appraisal, and environmental flags from a neighbouring property nobody checked. None of these are fatal — if they're addressed in the file before submission rather than discovered by the lender.
Our submissions reconcile every number and disclose every issue with a mitigation attached. That's why our GTA multi-residential files move through credit in days, not weeks.
